summary: Full-stack AI media generation app on the Internet Computer that creates images and videos using the Replicate API. Features a dark ultra-modern UI, unlimited credits (no credit cost per generation), personal gallery, admin panel, Stripe payments, and Internet Identity login.
architectural_notes: Runs on the Internet Computer (ICP) platform
Uses Replicate API for all AI media generation (images and videos)
known_issues: Media generation (image/video) fails with 'generation error' state in production — root cause under investigation (Replicate API key, timeouts, payload issues)

existing_features.title: Implement Internet Identity authentication so users can securely log in and log out. All protected features (generation, gallery, credits) must require authentication.

Build a personal gallery page where authenticated users can view all their previously generated images and videos. Each item should display its media, the prompt used, the generation type (image or video), and the creation timestamp.

Build an admin panel accessible only to a designated admin principal. The admin panel must display a list of all users with their principal IDs, credit balances, and total generations. Admins can manually adjust user credit balances.

Integrate Stripe payments on the frontend so users can purchase credit bundles. Display credit package options (e.g., 1000, 5000, 10000 credits). On successful Stripe payment confirmation, the backend increments the user's credit balance by the purchased amount.

Build a generation page with two tabs — Image and Video. Each tab has a prompt input field, a Generate button, a live status indicator (idle / processing / complete / error), and renders the result inline when complete. Show the user's current credit balance prominently and disable the Generate button if balance is below 500.

Apply a dark, ultra-modern visual theme across the entire app. Use a near-black background, high-contrast accent colors (avoid blue and purple), sharp typography, subtle glassmorphism or neon glow effects on cards and buttons, and a consistent design language across all pages.

Remove the low-credits warning and the disabled-generate-button logic from the generation page. The Generate button must always be enabled regardless of the user's credit balance, and no warning about insufficient credits should be shown.

Remove all credit-gate checks on the frontend. The Generate button must always be enabled regardless of the displayed credit balance. No low-credits warning, disabled state, or credit-threshold logic should appear anywhere in the UI.

Update the Admin page to include a Replicate API Key configuration section. Display the current key status (configured / not configured) via hasReplicateApiKey, provide a password-type text input and a Save button that calls setReplicateApiKey, and show a success or error toast on completion. If the key is not configured, display a prominent warning banner on the Admin page.

Implement client-side exponential backoff retry logic in the GenerationTab component. After a generation error, display a 'Retry' button that re-submits the same prompt up to 3 times with delays of 2s, 4s, and 8s between attempts. Show the current attempt number (e.g., 'Retrying… attempt 2/3') during retries. Use a stable idempotency key (derived from prompt + timestamp) stored in component state so the same request is not double-processed.

Improve the generation error UX on the GenerationTab component. When a typed error variant is returned from the backend (#ApiKeyMissing, #ApiError, #Timeout, #ParseError), display a specific, human-readable error message corresponding to the error type rather than a generic 'Generation error'. Map each variant to a distinct user-facing string (e.g., 'Service not configured — contact admin', 'Provider error — please retry', 'Request timed out — please retry', 'Unexpected response — please retry').

new_feature_requests.summary: Personal gallery to view generated media
Admin panel for managing users and content
Stripe payments integration for purchasing credits
Internet Identity login for secure authentication

Integrate the Replicate API on the backend to generate images. Expose a backend endpoint that accepts a text prompt, calls Replicate's image generation model, stores the resulting image URL in the user's gallery, and returns the result to the frontend.

Integrate the Replicate API on the backend to generate videos. Expose a backend endpoint that accepts a text prompt, calls Replicate's video generation model, stores the resulting video URL in the user's gallery, and returns the result to the frontend.
Remove credit limits — users have unlimited credits and are never blocked from generating

Remove all credit cost deductions on the backend when a user generates an image or video. Users should never have their credit balance decremented, and generation should never be blocked due to insufficient credits.

Remove all credit deduction logic from the backend. When a user generates an image or video, do not decrement their credit balance. Generation must never be blocked or rejected due to insufficient credits.

Audit and harden the backend Replicate API HTTP outcall for image generation: ensure the API key is read from a stable config store, the request payload matches Replicate's current REST schema (POST /v1/predictions with correct model version, input fields, and Content-Type: application/json), the IC HTTP outcall transform function is correctly stripping non-deterministic headers, and the response body is fully parsed before storing the result URL. Return a structured error variant (#ApiKeyMissing, #ApiError(text), #Timeout, #ParseError) instead of a generic trap so the frontend can display a meaningful message.

Audit and harden the backend Replicate API HTTP outcall for video generation using the same approach as REQ-14: correct payload schema, deterministic transform, typed error variants, and gallery storage before returning.

Add a Replicate API key configuration endpoint to the backend admin interface. The admin must be able to set (and update) the Replicate API key via a dedicated admin-only backend function (setReplicateApiKey). The key must be stored in stable memory so it survives canister upgrades. A companion query (hasReplicateApiKey: Bool) must allow the frontend to check whether the key is configured.
